Interface IoTIntegrationService

All Known Implementing Classes:
MockIoTIntegrationService, MqttIntegrationService
public interface IoTIntegrationService
Defines operations for optional MQTT integration with physical IoT devices.

This service encapsulates broker access behind a small interface, allowing the rest of the application to depend on this contract rather than the MQTT implementation details. This design enables both mock testing and real MQTT integration without coupling the rest of the application to a specific broker library.

Topic Convention:

  • State Topic: smarthome/<deviceId>/state - Device publishes its state here
  • Command Topic: smarthome/<deviceId>/cmd - App publishes commands here

    Nested Classes

    Interface
    Description
    static interface 
    IoTIntegrationService.DeviceStateChangeListener
    Callback interface for device state changes received from the broker.
    static final record 
    IoTIntegrationService.IoTConfiguration
    Configuration record for MQTT broker settings.

  • Method Details

    • getProtocolName

      String getProtocolName()
      Returns the protocol name (e.g., "MQTT").
      Returns:
      protocol name

    • getConfiguration

      IoTIntegrationService.IoTConfiguration getConfiguration()
      Returns the current IoT configuration.
      Returns:
      current configuration settings

    • isEnabled

      boolean isEnabled()
      Checks if IoT integration is enabled.
      Returns:
      true if enabled, false otherwise

    • isConnected

      boolean isConnected()
      Checks if currently connected to the MQTT broker.
      Returns:
      true if connected, false otherwise

    • getLastSync

      String getLastSync()
      Returns the timestamp of the last successful synchronization with the broker.
      Returns:
      timestamp string (or "Never" if not yet synchronized)

    • getDiscoveredDevices

      javafx.collections.ObservableList<IntegrationDevice> getDiscoveredDevices()
      Returns the list of discovered/paired integration devices.
      Returns:
      observable list of integration devices

    • saveConfiguration

      void saveConfiguration(boolean enabled, String broker, int port, String username, String password)
      Saves broker configuration settings.

      This method updates the configuration but does not automatically connect. Call connect() to establish a connection after saving.

      Parameters:
      enabled - whether to enable IoT integration
      broker - broker address
      port - broker port
      username - broker username (optional)
      password - broker password (optional)

    • testConnection

      boolean testConnection(String broker, String portValue)
      Tests if the given broker and port settings are valid.

      This performs validation without establishing an actual connection. For real implementations, this may attempt a brief connection.

      Parameters:
      broker - broker address to test
      portValue - broker port as a string
      Returns:
      true if parameters appear valid, false otherwise

    • connect

      boolean connect()
      Connects to the MQTT broker with current configuration.

      Connection is only attempted if integration is enabled and broker settings are valid. On successful connection, subscribes to all devices' state topics and updates lastSync.

      Returns:
      true if connection was successful, false otherwise

    • disconnect

      void disconnect()
      Disconnects from the MQTT broker and cleans up resources.

    • refreshDevices

      boolean refreshDevices()
      Refreshes the list of discovered devices from the broker.

      Only successful if currently connected.

      Returns:
      true if refresh was successful, false if not connected or error occurred

    • publishCommand

      boolean publishCommand(String deviceId, String command)
      Publishes a command to a device's command topic.

      The command is published to smarthome/<deviceId>/cmd. Only successful if connected and the device is paired.

      Parameters:
      deviceId - the device identifier
      command - command payload to publish
      Returns:
      true if published successfully, false otherwise

    • getDeviceState

      String getDeviceState(String deviceId)
      Retrieves the current state of a paired device.

      This may return a cached value if the device has been recently queried.

      Parameters:
      deviceId - the device identifier
      Returns:
      current state string (e.g., "ON", "OFF", temperature value), or null if not found

    • addStateChangeListener

      void addStateChangeListener(IoTIntegrationService.DeviceStateChangeListener listener)
      Adds a listener to be notified when a device's state changes via MQTT message.
      Parameters:
      listener - callback to invoke with deviceId and new state

    • removeStateChangeListener

      void removeStateChangeListener(IoTIntegrationService.DeviceStateChangeListener listener)
      Removes a previously registered state change listener.
      Parameters:
      listener - the listener to remove
